mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, month-over-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 52% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4% indicates sufficiently restrictive monetary policy that raises the cost of capital and depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 55% of instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate and increase in payroll employment signals accelerating labor market absorption that boosts wage income and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through a joint effect stronger than either variable alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE changes and PAYEMS changes fails to achieve a t-statistic greater than 2.0 in predicting 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, a contraction in industrial production exceeding 1% over three months signals an impending growth slowdown that triggers earnings downgrade risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months triggering INDPRO contraction during late-cycle expansion, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of cases over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1500 thousand units indicate strong residential investment and durable goods demand that lifts cyclical earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, the next 5-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of triggered months over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating household income and shrinking consumption demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: After UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp over 2 months, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in quarterly real GDP and monthly industrial production signals a broad-based recovery spanning aggregate demand and manufacturing supply, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days that exceed the predictive power of either proxy in isolation.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to produce a Spearman correlation above 0.05 with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising month-over-month industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and positive operating leverage that boosts corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 52% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.
